name: Day 55: it’s a two rattlesnake, bear, and wipeout kinda day
description:
Had the pleasure of having Suzie and Matt with me for 7 miles today since they were in town for a wedding! I met them right after this farmstand appeared out of nowhere on the side of the road (with incredible pumpkin bread). They brought me chocolate milk, fruit, and veggies, and carried my pack for a few miles, all of which felt miraculous! The day derailed a little bit from there - took a big spill off a rock, ran into two difficult rattlesnakes, then had two bear encounters, one of which wouldn’t leave our campsite alone. I had planned on a huge day of miles today, trying to get to Waynesboro (or at least close), but it was a good reminder that I can only control the controllables and everything else is just my own expectations. I got about 9 miles from Waynesboro, and I’ll just finish up those miles in the morning. No biggie! 

End mile: 856.5

name: Morning Hike
Day 53: silly days with great people
description: Did a few miles into town for what we expected to be an efficient in-and-out operation this morning. Instead, we were treated to the radical generosity of Buena Vista, VA! Free rides, a beautiful town square, a huge free ice cream at Holy Cow, and a leisurely few hours hanging at the sub shop and town square. We had originally planned to cowboy camp (no tents) on Cole Mountain, and have a cowboy themed night around it, but had to get very adaptable with the plan! It was insanely windy and we couldn’t build a fire, so we boiled Li’l smokies and ate cans of baked beans and watched the most gorgeous sunset and starry night we’ve had so far on trail. The wind is still picking up, so we’re in tents with no rain flies to be able to still see the stars tonight! Cheers to silly nights with great people :)

name: Day 52: first 10 by 10 & 800 miles!
description: Woke up today to hundreds of tiny ants swarming my bear can but was determined not to let it ruin my day (even though I’m not sure how it hasn’t happened before or how to stop it from happening again)! Today’s hike started through a post-wildfire section of the forest that was fairly spooky in the hot and windy morning. I did some of my fastest miles ever for the first 6-8 miles, then realized I might accomplish my first 10 by 10 despite starting late. 10 miles by 10am is a loose rule of thumb that seasoned thru-hikers start using when their mileage gets high and the days get hot - it helps get the bulk of the miles in before the heat of the day. For one reason or another, late mornings or slow miles or a 9am break, I’ve never managed it. Proud to do it today despite the ant fiasco this morning! I stopped after 10am for my “oatmeal with views” break, then pushed a huge 23 mile day, passing 800 miles, to end closer to tomorrow’s town day at a campsite near a creek!

name: Day 51: rumor has it I might be enjoying uphills these days
description:
Went to bed last night dreading the 4000+ feet of sustained climbing today, but somehow woke up ready to tackle uphills this morning when my alarm went off at 4:30! By the time I stopped for a real breakfast, I had already crushed 9 miles and almost 2700 feet of climbing. I made an early decision to do 20 miles instead of the 16 I had originally planned, and Horsepower and Genuine agreed. The weather was very kind to us and our early morning meant we could stop for a long lunch break and nap. Saw some cool flowers, a cool orb (weather observatory, looks like a World Cup ad to me!), and some cool views! Dinner was new - swapped my ramen/couscous for rice this time, and added a sundried tomato ricotta pesto in a poor man’s approximation of my mom’s tuna casserole. Delicious! 

End mile: 780.4

name: Day 50: helloooo blue ridge parkway!
description:
Started the morning with a divine little stretch of the Blue Ridge Parkway. Only a handful of cars, the deer, the sunrise, and me! We’re planning to shift our schedule a little earlier to take advantage of the cooler mornings, so I started hiking at 6am and stopped for breakfast around 8 at one of the overlooks on the parkway! The terrain was mellow and the weather was mild, so I 
 cruised for a good 15 first miles - some solo, some with Horsepower. Feet started to hurt pretty badly again around mile 16, but felt better after a break and had no problem climbing 1000 feet to our campsite for an early dinner before another early morning!

name: Day 49: cruisin’ down easy street, 1/3 of the way to Maine!
description:
Had a long slow morning at BeeChill Hostel! Beth (the owner) made an incredible hiker breakfast of egg casserole and Belgian waffles with ice cream. We did chores, repackaged resupply, washed gear, and lazed around eating fresh fruit and veggies until around noon. We started hiking and immediately hit the 1/3 to Katahdin sign! Next huge fraction milestone will be the halfway mark, which is crazy. Had an easy short day of hiking with some of the most gorgeous breezy weather we’ve had so far, so no complaints from me - I’d like more of this, please! 

End mile: 742.3

name: Day 48: no pain no gain!
description:
Woke up at 4am to catch the sunrise on McAfee Knob, a huge AT milestone and the most photographed spot on the Appalachian Trail! We ate breakfast and watched the sunrise, then hiked a grueling 20 miles in blazing heat (Virginia is experiencing record highs for June temps)! The day definitely wore on all of us a bit - we were pushing to get into town one day early, and our originally planned easy day had suddenly become extremely long and wildly hot. Wild blueberries and blackberries kept me entertained, and I led the slog until about 3 miles outside of town, when my feet totally gave up. Every step hurt the soles of my feet! We stopped in at a gas station for some chocolate milk with one mile to go, and I called Noah for some distraction and comfort. We finally made it to BeeCh Hill, a rustic little hostel with the kindest host on earth. She drove us into town for more food than a single person should ever eat in one sitting, and picked us up at the grocery store post-resupply! 

End mile: 732.1

name: Day 47: “this mountain’s a buckin’ bronco, but I ain’t never been bucked!” - Horsepower
description:
Huge monster day of climbing and mileage today, and I’m pretty damn proud of myself! After 3 huge days, doing a 22 mile day with almost 6,000 feet of elevation gain is no joke. Horsepower finally caught up with me, and it was nice to have some hiking company over the huge climbs before getting to the first of three famous Virginia sights (the VA triple crown), Dragon’s Tooth! Some diabolical rock scrambles coming down from there - it took us almost an hour to go a mile and it made for a very long day. We also crossed the 700 mile marker and ran into a herd of cows (and a bull)! The day wore very long as we pushed closer to McAfee knob - trying to camp close to it so we can see it at sunrise - but we finally made it to a shelter nearby with aching feet and exhausted legs. A full and satisfying day!

End mile: 712.3

name: Day 46: some people treat their body like a temple, I treat mine like a tent
description:
In other words, get it dirty and test it against the forces of nature! 

My body was definitely tested today on my third consecutive big mileage day. The views were gorgeous - open pastures, a 300(!) year old oak tree named the Keffer Oak, hay bales, and mountain ridges. We also passed the Eastern Continental Divide - water that falls west of here goes to the Gulf of Mexico, while water that falls east of here goes to the Atlantic Ocean! I just had one big-ish climb today, so I was pumped to get to camp early, but as soon as I walked into camp the sky turned black and the wind started to howl. I barely had time to stake down my tent (with John’s help; the wind was whipping it around too much) before a terrifying thunderstorm hit. Luckily it only lasted a little over an hour, but I cooked dinner in my tent, played music, and waited it out. I ended up spending 15(!) hours in my tent before I stepped out in the morning, which feels a little insane. Hoping this helps our dry water sources a little bit! 

End mile: 691.0

name: Day 45: dare I say we’re… back?
description:
Started to feel better and more like myself today! Today’s terrain consisted of two massive climbs with the most diabolical ridge walk over a rock field (see third picture for an example of the terrain that continued for MILES - slow going and painful on the feet)! The views in the middle were worth it, and you could see McAfee Knob (our destination in a few days) in the distance! The last climb might’ve been the hardest so far on trail - 1600 feet straight up with the last 500 feet to the campsite basically vertical! See sweaty proof. Super proud of both climbs, but especially that last one. Dinner never tasted so good! 

End mile: 673.5

name: Day 44: another tough one!
description:
Stopped in Pearisburg, VA (a town just a mile or so off trail) for resupply this morning. Which made it all the more impressive that we fully resupplied and still managed a 20-mile day! It was a pretty uneventful day, but we got caught in a MASSIVE thunderstorm a little bit before lunch. Luckily the sun came out at lunchtime and we were able to lay out our gear (and ourselves, for a little nap)! Virginia has been SUPER dry (i.e. every water source has been dry and it’s super stressful) so that’s been a new challenge to adjust to and makes me a little more tolerant of the rainstorms. Last picture is of our massive bear hang - I’m a bear can girl myself, but I’ve had so much extra food with this big six-day food carry that John has been kindly hanging whatever doesn’t fit up with his! 

End mile: 653.0

name: Day 43: just keep walking
description:
Still pretty sick and congested, and didn’t sleep very well, so I lazed around the paradise that is Woods Hole Hostel until around 3pm after a homemade breakfast of eggs, a Hindu dish called Sabudana Khichdi, French toast casserole, homemade bread, apple butter, etc. It was absolutely incredible! The hostel owner, Neville, even left us in charge of checking in hikers while she left for lunch since we had been there for so long. 
Once I started hiking, I started feeling the effects of being sick, not sleeping well, and eating too little for lunch. It took everything in me to hike 9 rainy miles to my destination, looking like Quasimodo in my poncho, but sometimes that’s just the way it is. Set up camp and had one of my favorite dehydrated meals and am hoping for a better day tomorrow!

End mile: 634.4

name: Day 42: high risk high reward!
description:
Said goodbye to Noah and my dad this morning (don’t ask how many tears were shed and how long I made them park by the trailhead before I was ready to say goodbye) (it was like an hour) and ended up getting on trail wayyy later than expected - it was after noon when I started! I was going to push as far as I could and meet Horsepower and Genuine tomorrow, after their stay at Woods Hole hostel, then realized I could just push hard and stay there with them tonight. I did 21 miles after 12:15pm, which is crazy and I’m very proud of myself for it! Wildlife went crazy today - 2 snakes, a turtle, 2 bears, and a bunch of deer! 

As soon as I walked into Woods Hole, I knew I made the right decision. The hostel owner, Neville, had a plate leftover from dinner in the fridge for me, Genuine was drawing a picture of our little trail crew, and it just felt so truly AT that I felt like I had been pulled there by the trail itself. 

End mile: 625.8

name: Day 41: zero day!
description: Technically zero trail miles today! Had a wonderful morning taking my dad and noah back to the Grayson Highlands to see the wild ponies.

name: Day 40: 20 miles with my running buddy!
description:
Slackpacked 20 miles today with Noah! I hadn’t run in over 45 days, so I had a blast throwing on a running vest and clocking some faster miles with my favorite person. Got to hike a little bit with my dad, too, before he drove around to the end to pick us up with cold waters and gatorades. Ended the day getting dinner with Dreamcatcher, my OG trail friend, after she hiked into Marion!

End mile: 605.2

name: Day 39: are you kidding me with this sunrise
description:
An incredible day. Woke up to the most spectacular sunrise of my life and got to watch it from my cozy sleeping quilt! Had a slow morning up on Chestnut Knob before heading out for a medium day, but ended up having to speed through my miles a little bit to get to the road where my dad and Noah were picking me up!! Took a little dip in the creek there with Noah (so I didn’t stink up the whole car with my 10 day unshowered self) and it was cold and amazing. Horsepower, John, and I devoured a watermelon via plastic spoon courtesy of Noah and my dad, and then we headed to our Airbnb, where Noah made us his incredible smashburgers. Might I call this a perfect day? 

End mile: 585.2

name: Day 38: it’s the cliiiiimb
description:
Hot hot day in the sun! Hiked some meadows, took sunny naps, forded a river (and ate lunch in it), hiked a massive climb up to the shelter, and saw all manner of butterflies, snakes, and deer… all to end up at the best campsite views so far on trail. Sunset over the valley and a gentle breeze - this place felt like heaven. We played some Uno (cards cut in half to save weight of course) and mostly just stared in awe at the sunset. Wow.

End mile: 570.1

name: Day 37: all roads lead to Mexican food
description:
We are 1/4 of the way to Maine!! Woke up rested and ready to rip both yesterday’s and today’s miles! We hit some trail magic out of a one room schoolhouse, but our only thought all morning was a Mexican restaurant right on the trail as you cross a highway. After hiking through some meadows, we made it to El Burrito Loco along with most of our little bubble of hikers. Smashed a quesadilla and a basket of chips and kept moving! Passed through a cow pasture and found a huge jawbone (thank you Horsepower for the ID) and made it to a huge campsite that we had just to the three of us! Tried a new meal - Walmart chicharrón with salsa verde and Knorr dehydrated rice - and loved it! We finally went to sleep despite a whippoorwill chirping at full volume all night and me threatening to shoot it. A classic day on the AT! 

End mile: 552.8

name: Day 36: town day turned lazy nero!
description:
Woke up at 4:30 to hike 6.5 miles and catch the 8:45am free shuttle into Marion, VA. Super disciplined start to what ended up being a veeery lazy day! Resupplied at Walmart (especially my new obsession - Fritos Honey BBQ Twists), got some sandwiches, met Benjamin the giant tortoise, and hung out at the town outfitter for way too long. When we finally shuttled back to trail, it was pouring, and were we really going to get wet just to hike three miles that we could just hike in the morning? Nope! We set up camp right there and happily fell asleep having done only 6.5 miles on the day. 

End mile: 534.6

name: Day 35: we pay our dues for yesterday’s views
description:
For every view we had yesterday, all we got today were never ending woods. That’s okay! The dull days make it worth it. We woke up to an insanely windy and foggy morning that almost ripped my tent out of the ground. Hiked solo for the first half of the day and listened to an audiobook, then joined the boys for the afternoon. All three of us were really dead on our feet at the end of the day and the last mile to the campsite felt like forever. But the last day before town day is Buffet Night - we traded food, ate everything we had, made new flavor combinations, and basically tried to empty our food stores before hitting town tomorrow!

End mile: 528.0

name: Day 34: greatest trail day ever feat. wild ponies!
description:
Had the best day so far on trail - friends, weather, wild ponies, and some of the most incredible views I’ve ever seen! Ran into a couple of ponies right out of the gate that were using me as a salt lick. Crossed the 500(!!) mile marker, then entered the Grayson Highlands, which was miles of meadows and ponies that had no fear of us. We set up camp on a ridge overlooking the valley and were treated to an incredible sunset to cap off the day. 

End mile: 508.8

name: Day 33: the one where I finally start stretching
description: Fairly uneventful day! Hiked a bunch of miles with John, who’s great about stretching 2 or 3 times a day. I’ve been dealing with some pretty bad back/neck pain and the beginnings of some plantar fasciitis, so I decided to follow his lead and felt a lot better! We started a new habit of putting our feet up (and maybe dozing off a little bit) at 2nd lunch, and I packed out a glass jar of pesto (heavy!) in my last resupply and have been adding it to my dinners, which has been spectacular.

name: Day 32: Damascus, VA and a reunion!
description: Started my day just a few miles outside of Damascus, VA, which prides itself on being a trail town! Finally got my thru-hiker hang tag from the Appalachian Trail Conservancy (I started too late in the season to get one in Georgia), which was a minor thing but felt like a huge deal! I also reunited with Horsepower, who I hiked almost two weeks with on trail but hadn’t seen in almost as long while his sister was visiting. Did some laundry, carried a bag of grapes around town, ate a huge bowl of ice cream, then finally got moving! The AT is closed for a stretch due to severe damage from Hurricane Helene in 2024, so I took the official trail detour, which will rejoin the AT in about 20 miles. Ended the day with John at a campsite and a new flavor of dehydrated meal!

name: Day 31: HELLOOOO VIRGINIA!
description:
Out of Tennessee and into Virginia! It’s hard to believe that I already made it to my fourth state on the Appalachian Trail. Virginia is the longest state at 500+ miles and I’ll be here for about a month. Had an absolutely gorgeous hot day and smooth sailing on easy terrain today. If this is what Virginia is like… bring it on! 

End mile: 468.8

name: Day 30: a new kind of wet
description:
Rain held off, so instead I was just humid wet instead of rain wet! Started off my morning with coffee (if you know me, I know your jaw dropped) and Captain Crunch at the hostel. It was hard to leave such a cool spot! Today was almost too wet and humid to take any pictures, but I had a solid 16 miles of climbing to a shelter. Crossed the 20% mark today, and will enter Virginia tomorrow, which I can barely believe! 

End mile: 444.8

name: Day 29: surprise near-o!
description:
Surprise! Was supposed to take a zero day, but I didn’t like the hostel I was at, so I made a gut decision to take a near-o instead of a zero and move to a hostel 7 miles further (Boots Off) and I’m SO glad. More hikers, true AT culture, and great amenities! I also started the day with a wonderful care package from my parents with my new shoes, so I was truly in a great mood. It was only made better when Dreamcatcher, my friend from the first two weeks, texted me to invite me to dinner with her parents, who were staying in an Airbnb nearby. It was so needed after a few too many days of solitude. Also check out Dreamcatcher’s blog on The Trek! https://thetrek.co/author/charlotte-rice/

End mile: 429

name: Day 28: 4 weeks in!
description:
Suuuper easy day today! 12 miles with barely any climbing, gorgeous views of Laurel Falls, and mostly sunny after pouring the entire night. Got some incredible trail magic at the gap before the falls, and walked into Hampton for a resupply/stay!

End mile: 422.9

name: Day 27: clear skies, fully belly, can’t lose
description:
Started the day out with the most famous breakfast on the Appalachian Trail (AT’s best breakfast 13 years running or something like that)! Had some easy terrain today, mostly sunny with a few little drizzles here and there. And I passed the 400 mile marker! Legs feel good after the monster 25-miler yesterday, but I’m ready for a zero day!

End mile: 411.5

name: Day 26: toughest day yet
description:
Watch died… this was actually 25 miles! Several hours of torrential downpours, my longest day so far on trail, and the coldest I’ve been so far! Definitely shed some tears today, but trail magic kept showing up - strangers who made me tea on trail, offered extra layers, and hiked a few miles with me. And couldn’t have been a bigger turnaround than arriving at Mountain Harbour B&B. We’re tenting for free behind the B&B just to take advantage of their breakfast, kitchen (look at that burger), and Memorial Day live music! 

End mile: 395.6

name: Day 25: rainy but friendly
description:
Started the day hiking with Whack-a-Mole, past the diabolically steep Hurricane Helene detour, and found some new little orange friends! Made it to the shelter before the evening rain hit, just in time to help build a fire and try to dry out. Tried a new recipe for dinner (boil in a bag couscous - 10/10) and slept in a shelter for the first time since the smokies. 

End mile: 370.8

name: Day 24: rainy and lonely
description:
Very nearly took a zero day at Uncle Johnny’s hostel, but decided to stick to my original plan and take a zero next week. Opted to bandit over the train trestle instead of taking the two hour hurricane detour and got DRENCHED while doing so! Hiked a fairly lonely day but enjoyed myself until the second downpour hit, and I decided to just keep hiking until the rain stopped instead of doing a short day. Was incredibly grateful to run into Whack-a-Mole (my first friend from day 2 of trail!) at a campsite and decided to drop anchor for the night. 

End mile: 357.9

name: Day 23: civilization!
description:
Started out the day witnessing some of the devastation from Helene even two years later, which hit hard. Entire forests leveled and hundred year old trees tossed around. Made me extremely grateful for the people who maintain the trail and for the forest that remains. Unlocked a new animal (toads) before getting to Erwin, TN for a town meal and a dip in the river! 

End mile: 344,6

name: Day 22: hello again TN, trail magic, bald mountain, deer!
description:
Huge hot day of climbing today! I’ve been in and out of Tennessee for a few days now but this is the first sign I’ve seen. Got some trail magic in the form of clementines and Gatorade just before the climb up Big Bald Mountain, which literally brought me to tears. It was so breathtaking (not just because of the climb!) and I felt like the only person on earth. Then sunset and some deer at Bald Mountain Shelter to close out the night! 

End mile: 327.7

name: Day 21: feeling like Hansel and Gretel
description:
300+ miles and 3 weeks down!! Stopped by the Southern Cookie Lady’s house, which was literally a fairy tale - she lives right off trail and bakes cookies for hikers! Hiked an incredible ridge and then wrapped up my first 20 miler of the trail with some unfortunately spicy pad thai and a Nutella and potato chip dessert.

End mile: 308.8

name: Day 20! goodbyes, snake (!!), and another fire tower!
description:
Shorter day but still some big climbs! Hiked 5ish miles with Noah Cracknell fueled by Joey’s Bagels (and almost knocked him down trying to get away from a snake), then 7.5 more to a dispersed tentsite. Also, cosmic brownies were a GREAT resupply move. Feeling a little low after the goodbye with Noah and separating from my trail family for a little while, but we’ll be good in the morning! 

End mile: 288.0

name: Day 19: a visitor! and mile 275!
description:
Had a gorgeous day of mostly downhill into the little trail town of Hot Springs, NC - the trail runs right down their Main Street! Earned a big burger, fried pickles, hummus wrap, and some beers at Spring Creek Tavern and watched my worlds collide as I introduced Noah to my little trail family. 

End mile: 275.4

name: Day 18: a shower, a dog, an alien building, and a visit from my favorite person!
description:
Earned my visit from Noah Cracknell with a monster day of climbing - almost 6,000 feet of elevation gain! Walked under I-40, took an outdoor shower and mini-resupply at Standing Bear Farm, had lunch at the alien headquarters, then finished off the day introducing Noah to my backcountry dinner concoctions! 

End mile: 257.1

name: Day 17: see ya Smokies!
description:
Smokies, you were something. You were freezing cold, hot and sunny, quiet sometimes, a party other times, gave us bears and deer and rattlesnakes, and a whole lot of climbing, but incredible views. Happy to be through with ya 

End mile: 240.4

name: Day 16: frolicking and playing
description:
Some days you do 10 miles by 10am, some days you do a single mile and then play on some rocks. Have a little fun with it! Also notable: new ingredient hit the classic PB&J, I had several courses for dinner at 3:30pm (including a dessert burrito), and the low was in the 20s overnight!!

End mile: 223.7

name: Day 15: all roads lead to Cici’s pizza / a town full of trail magic
description:
Hard to do this perfect day justice. Woke up at 4:40am to hike Kuwohi, the highest point on the Appalachian Trail, at sunrise. Hiked a few more hours (crossing the 200 mile mark!) and then hitchhiked into Gatlinburg for an all you can eat pizza and salad buffet, and our hitch went in ahead of us and paid for our meals! The laundromat pulled out loaner clothes and free detergent for us, the outfitter gave us a room to hang out and store our backpacks during the day, and then we got the world’s coolest hitchhike back into the Smokies right when we were starting to lose hope. The trail really does provide, and the people here are SO incredibly generous! 

End mile: 211.1

name: Day 14: back and forth between TN/NC
description:
Honestly the most normal day so far on trail until we arrived at a shelter to a man sharpening the biggest machete I’ve ever seen! Luckily he used it to build a fire, which was a godsend for the frigid night we got

End mile: 197.5

name: Day 13: God’s country? No. Bear country.
description:
Head on a swivel for bears in the Smokies but saw none until our extremely persistent friend at Russell Fields shelter. I was somehow nominated to be on bear duty so I stayed strapped with rocks in one hand and bear spray in the other! Kept the bear away until nighttime and then woke up to a chomp mark in my shoe. Thank you Mr. Bear 

End mile: 181.1

name: Day 12: to the edge of the smokies!
description:
Pretty uneventful day, but walked 18 miles, shuttled into Fontana Village for a burger, and am camping at the edge of the Smokies! 

End mile: 166.2

name: Day 11: just happy to be here!
description:
Don’t be fooled by the short mileage, this was so hard! Started my day with a diabolical 4000 foot climb out of the NOC with my heaviest pack yet - I’m carrying 5 days of food to make it through the first half of the smokies, and my pack is 34 pounds! Was a beautiful day though and caught Sam a few times at viewpoints for a chat. Camping tonight and then to the Smokies tomorrow ⛰️⛰️

End mile: 147.7

name: Day 10: we’re so back
description: Woke up after the Worst Night Of All Time™ to a foggy cold day for the first 8 miles. Fog burned off and the skies cleared for the most incredible views of the trail so far, plus some trail magic from Pastor Steve and a stop for the night at the NOC!

name: Day 9: the one where everything went wrong
description: Holy hard day. Made a quick town stop into Franklin, NC for a resupply and my first vegetable of the trail in an attempt to beat scurvy. And thennn the day went so downhill after that! Torrential downpour, gunshots (?), lightning and thunder, and 12 miles to an absolutely packed campsite where every attempt to dry my clothes was unsuccessful. Also, I fell off a log mid-pee and almost fell off a cliff. Sweet!

name: Day 8: 100 miles complete!
description: Almost my first 20 miler! Easy-ish terrain until a crazy climb up to the fire tower on Albert Mountain that marks the first 100 miles of the Appalachian Trail. Hiking felt so good today and the weather was gorgeous but we’re about to pay the price in the rain tomorrow!

name: Day 7: see ya GA, hello NC!
description: Had the butterflies for company today on a long solo 18 miles! Took me on a blue blaze to the wreckage of a plane crash, and finally met back up with Charlotte and Sam at Standing Indian for an incredible sunset and a great tent city!

name: Day 6: last full day in GA!
description:
Slackpacked the 17 miles between Unicoi Gap and Dick’s Creek Gap and felt light as a feather and even got a little running in there! It’s just hard to watch the miles go by so slowly when I’m used to clicking off a mile in less than 10 minutes and now they take 20-30. Especially when I’m hiking alone! But I’m back at Stanimals for one more night and then I’ll cross over to North Carolina tomorrow!

End mile: 69.3

name: Day 5
description: 10 miles done and dusted before noon! First hostel stay tonight and I have never been more excited for a shower, a frozen pizza, and the Celtics

name: Day 4
description:
Long day! Saw the shoe tree at Neels Gap (where people who quit the trail on day 3 throw their shoes up), did a mini resupply at Mountain Crossings at Walasi-Yi, and rejoined my new friends from yesterday back at Low Gap Shelter!

End mile: 43

name: Day 3
description:
Survived a scary thunderstorm in my tent last night and was rewarded with low humidity, stunning views, and some new friends today!

End mile: 27.7

name: Day 2
description:
This is hard man

End mile: 13.6

name: Day 1
description:
The approach trail to the start of the Appalachian Trail being 8 miles is actually incredibly on brand

End mile: 1

name: Morning Run
Bittersweet!
description: One last Sunny Girl with the Sunny King

name: GMMMMM
description: Last Boston endorphins star jump :( 🌟🌟
